creator: josef.c.bailey@protonmail.com
text:
(In reply to Alexander Ziaee from comment #7)

For some reason when i run that command then run cat /dev/sndstat i get 0 >0 meaning it didn't do anything and dmesg dosen't show anything. Here is sysctl -a |grep hda

joe@freeBSD:~ $ sysctl -a |grep hda
hdac0: <Intel Comet Lake-LP HDA Controller> mem 0x9f914000-0x9f917fff,0x9f800000-0x9f8fffff at device 31.3 on pci0
hdacc0: <Intel Kaby Lake HDA CODEC> at cad 2 on hdac0
hdaa0: <Intel Kaby Lake Audio Function Group> at nid 1 on hdacc0
pcm0: <Intel Kaby Lake (HDMI/DP 8ch)> at nid 3 on hdaa0
device	snd_hda
irq130: hdac0:263 @cpu0(domain0): 5

Hope this helps
